cohort: • Subject: Updated HIV/HTLV benchmark using human-inclusive competitive alignment
I completed the rerun using a combined hg38 and retroviral reference, with stricter mapping filters and coordinate-
based deduplication. The updated figures summarize the workflow and results.
In the targeted HTLV cohort, 66/71 samples had strong HTLV1 signal, four had low signal, and one appeared to be a
sequencing/QC failure. HIV1, HIV2, HTLV2, and LINE1 background was eliminated. Only three borderline HERV fragments
remained at MAPQ 40, and all disappeared at MAPQ 60.
For WGS, HIV1 signal was retained in 2/37 HIV-labelled samples and in 0/23 HL controls. Each positive sample contained
only one high-quality HIV1 fragment with approximately 1.5–1.8% genome breadth, so I would describe these as single-
fragment candidates rather than definitive detections. No HIV or HTLV signal was observed in the HL controls.
Adding hg38 eliminated the previously ubiquitous HERV/LINE1 counts, indicating that most of those reads were better
explained by human-genome mapping. Overall, the revised workflow provides substantially improved specificity while
The next useful benchmark will be the targeted HIV cohort when those data become available.
